agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "TOCTOU race condition allows overselling; cart add and stock decrement are non-atomic"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU race condition between stock check and decrement",
      "risk": "Two concurrent requests both read stock=1, both pass the check, both decrement — stock goes negative, overselling occurs",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() lines 1-3 (getStock → check → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 3. Before A's decrementStock executes, User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — also reads stock=1, passes check. 4. Both decrement. Stock is now -1. Two users 'own' one unit.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic conditional update: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $qty WHERE id = $id AND stock >= $qty`. Check affected rows — if 0, throw insufficient stock. This eliminates the read-then-write gap entirely.",
      "time_to_fix": "15-30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity, order fulfillment accuracy"
    },
    {
      "title": "Non-atomic cart add + stock decrement leaves inconsistent state on partial failure",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop), the cart contains an item but stock was never reserved — leading to overselling or ghost cart items",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() lines 2-3 (addItem then decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. addItem succeeds, writing item to cart. 2. decrementStock throws (network error, deadlock, timeout). 3. Promise rejects, but cart row persists. 4. Stock is not decremented — another user can buy the same unit. 5. At checkout, both users believe they have the item.",
      "fix": "Wrap both operations in a database transaction: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })`. If either fails, both roll back. The atomic stock update from Finding 1 should be the first operation inside the transaction — reserve stock before touching the cart.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency, checkout reliability"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "quantity=0, quantity=-5, or non-existent productId/userId are not validated, allowing stock manipulation (negative decrement increases stock) or meaningless cart entries",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart() function signature / entry",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -10). Stock check: stock < -10 is false (passes). addItem adds -10 qty. decrementStock with -10 effectively increments stock by 10. Attacker has manufactured inventory.",
      "fix": "Add guards at function entry: `if (!userId || !productId) throw new Error('Invalid IDs'); if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Quantity must be a positive integer');`",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security, data integrity"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider a SELECT FOR UPDATE or advisory lock if your DB doesn't support conditional atomic updates",
    "Add an idempotency key to prevent duplicate cart additions from retried requests",
    "Log failed stock reservations with productId and requested quantity for demand analytics"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Current code will oversell under any concurrent load — this is not a theoretical risk, it is a guaranteed production failure for popular products",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — inline function, no migration involved",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix is backward compatible; same function signature, stricter internal behavior",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned — add alerts for negative stock values and failed decrements",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "No deployment artifact to roll back — fix must be applied before shipping"
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition is deterministic under concurrent access — not speculative",
      "Partial failure between addItem/decrementStock is a standard non-transactional consistency bug",
      "Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Unknown whether db.products.decrementStock already has internal guards (e.g., CHECK constraint, atomic CAS) — if so, Finding 1 severity drops but Finding 2 remains"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "decrementStock is a naive UPDATE stock = stock - qty without a WHERE stock >= qty guard",
      "No database transaction wraps the caller of this function",
      "No application-level distributed lock exists upstream"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking: Classic TOCTOU race condition in cart stock management.

content.text:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "TOCTOU race condition allows overselling; cart add and stock decrement are non-atomic"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU race condition between stock check and decrement",
      "risk": "Two concurrent requests both read stock=1, both pass the check, both decrement — stock goes negative, overselling occurs",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() lines 1-3 (getStock → check → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 3. Before A's decrementStock executes, User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — also reads stock=1, passes check. 4. Both decrement. Stock is now -1. Two users 'own' one unit.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic conditional update: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $qty WHERE id = $id AND stock >= $qty`. Check affected rows — if 0, throw insufficient stock. This eliminates the read-then-write gap entirely.",
      "time_to_fix": "15-30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity, order fulfillment accuracy"
    },
    {
      "title": "Non-atomic cart add + stock decrement leaves inconsistent state on partial failure",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop), the cart contains an item but stock was never reserved — leading to overselling or ghost cart items",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart() lines 2-3 (addItem then decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. addItem succeeds, writing item to cart. 2. decrementStock throws (network error, deadlock, timeout). 3. Promise rejects, but cart row persists. 4. Stock is not decremented — another user can buy the same unit. 5. At checkout, both users believe they have the item.",
      "fix": "Wrap both operations in a database transaction: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })`. If either fails, both roll back. The atomic stock update from Finding 1 should be the first operation inside the transaction — reserve stock before touching the cart.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency, checkout reliability"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "quantity=0, quantity=-5, or non-existent productId/userId are not validated, allowing stock manipulation (negative decrement increases stock) or meaningless cart entries",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart() function signature / entry",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -10). Stock check: stock < -10 is false (passes). addItem adds -10 qty. decrementStock with -10 effectively increments stock by 10. Attacker has manufactured inventory.",
      "fix": "Add guards at function entry: `if (!userId || !productId) throw new Error('Invalid IDs'); if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Quantity must be a positive integer');`",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security, data integrity"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider a SELECT FOR UPDATE or advisory lock if your DB doesn't support conditional atomic updates",
    "Add an idempotency key to prevent duplicate cart additions from retried requests",
    "Log failed stock reservations with productId and requested quantity for demand analytics"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Current code will oversell under any concurrent load — this is not a theoretical risk, it is a guaranteed production failure for popular products",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — inline function, no migration involved",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix is backward compatible; same function signature, stricter internal behavior",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned — add alerts for negative stock values and failed decrements",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "No deployment artifact to roll back — fix must be applied before shipping"
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition is deterministic under concurrent access — not speculative",
      "Partial failure between addItem/decrementStock is a standard non-transactional consistency bug",
      "Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Unknown whether db.products.decrementStock already has internal guards (e.g., CHECK constraint, atomic CAS) — if so, Finding 1 severity drops but Finding 2 remains"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "decrementStock is a naive UPDATE stock = stock - qty without a WHERE stock >= qty guard",
      "No database transaction wraps the caller of this function",
      "No application-level distributed lock exists upstream"
    ]
  }
}
